response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the profile diverges sharply from this role's hard requirements. There is no indication of Japanese language ability (native-level required), professional C-for-UNIX experience is limited to a brief stint in 2014-2015, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel source modification, embedded networking, LAN-switch products, or security technologies such as MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection. Strong general engineering craft does not offset the missing domain-critical qualifications.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at high request volumes in DLang
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for this position
Minimal C/UNIX depth and no Linux kernel analysis or modification experience
No background in network security features (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development

key_factors.factor: General systems and low-latency engineering skill
